source: SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI) daily holdings xlsx, ssga.com snapshot fetched 2026-04-19
survivorship_warning: This list is a point-in-time snapshot of the SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI) holdings as of the snapshot date. Biotech is a brutally selection-biased industry: the vast majority of small/mid-cap biotechs that existed in 2010 are now delisted (failed trials, bankruptcy, buyouts), and a frozen 2026 holdings list contains only the survivors. This is the highest survivorship-bias universe in the repo by a wide margin. The flip side is that biotech is also the most idiosyncratic equity sector - pricing is driven by binary trial outcomes and FDA decisions, which are nearly orthogonal to broad-market factors. A strategy that wins here without loading on momentum/beta is more likely to be a real single-name edge. Documented, not fixed.
